headline: Honda Warns of $15.7 Billion EV Charge as Auto Business Losses Deepen
summary: This article first appeared on GuruFocus.  Honda Motor Co. (NYSE:HMC) is confronting a sharp financial setback after an aggressive electric-vehicle push backfired, forcing the automaker to record a 2.5 trillion ($15.7 billion) impairment charge tied largely to EV investments that were abandoned shortly before launch.  The write-down could precede what may become Honda's first annual loss on record and highlights the risks of its late attempt to catch up with EV leaders such as Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and China's BYD (BYDDF).

raw.headline: Honda Warns of $15.7 Billion EV Charge as Auto Business Losses Deepen
raw.summary: This article first appeared on GuruFocus.  Honda Motor Co. (NYSE:HMC) is confronting a sharp financial setback after an aggressive electric-vehicle push backfired, forcing the automaker to record a 2.5 trillion ($15.7 billion) impairment charge tied largely to EV investments that were abandoned shortly before launch.  The write-down could precede what may become Honda's first annual loss on record and highlights the risks of its late attempt to catch up with EV leaders such as Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and China's BYD (BYDDF).

headline: Uber, Nvidia plan robotaxi rollout in 28 cities starting next year
summary: Uber Technologies and Nvidia said on Monday they will deploy a fleet of robotaxis powered by Nvidia's autonomous driving software ‌on the ride-hailing network, starting in Los Angeles and San Francisco ‌in 2027 and expanding to 28 cities globally by 2028.  Alphabet's Waymo currently operates the ‌most advanced commercial robotaxi service, ⁠running fully driverless rides in cities including Phoenix, San Francisco and Los Angeles, and steadily expanding its fleet.  Uber and Nvidia said the ​vehicles ​will run on the DRIVE Hyperion autonomous ​vehicle platform along with Alpamayo, a ‌reasoning-based AI model designed to handle complex road scenarios.

Alphabet's Waymo currently operates the ‌most advanced commercial robotaxi service, ⁠running fully driverless rides in cities including Phoenix, San Francisco and Los Angeles, and steadily expanding its fleet.  Uber and Nvidia said the ​vehicles ​will run on the DRIVE Hyperion autonomous ​vehicle platform along with Alpamayo, a ‌reasoning-based AI model designed to handle complex road scenarios.

headline: Tesla’s ‘Cybercab’ Name Hits a Roadblock: A French Beverage Company
summary: Tesla  has made a pitch for a radical new era of “amazing abundance” in which its driverless Cybercabs ferry people around town while humanoid robots do most of the work.  Tesla is in a legal battle against an obscure drink wholesaler called Unibev that has claimed the rights to the term “Cybercab.”  The fight coincides with the launch of Tesla’s Cybercab, which is seen as key to the company’s autonomous vehicle and robotics ambitions.
